course_info.title: UX Design Internship
course_info.meeting_schedule: On-site at internship employer, weekly faculty check-in

learning_objectives: Define learning goals for the internship aligned with career development
Establish communication protocols with internship supervisor and faculty advisor
Create a structured learning plan with measurable milestones
readings: The First 90 Days by Michael Watkins, Ch. 1-2
Apprenticeship Patterns by Hoover & Oshineye, Ch. 1-2

lecture_notes.introduction: The internship is your bridge between academic learning and professional practice. Success depends on setting clear goals, building relationships, and documenting your growth throughout the experience.
key_concepts: SMART learning goals: specific, measurable, achievable, relevant, time-bound
Stakeholder mapping at your internship: supervisor, team, cross-functional partners
The learning plan: skills to develop, projects to pursue, feedback to seek
Weekly reflection practice: documenting learnings, challenges, and growth
Professional communication: asking good questions, seeking feedback proactively
lecture_notes.demonstration: Goal-setting workshop: each student writes 3 SMART learning goals for their internship and presents them for peer and faculty feedback

review.value: Specific: 'Conduct 3 moderated usability test sessions independently by week 8.' Not: 'Get better at user research.' The specificity enables measurement and accountability.
Do not wait for performance reviews. Ask your supervisor weekly: 'What is one thing I could improve?' and after each project: 'What went well and what could I do differently next time?'

learning_objectives: Adapt academic UX skills to professional workplace contexts
Navigate team dynamics, design critiques, and cross-functional collaboration
Document and reflect on the gap between academic and professional practice
readings: Design is a Job by Mike Monteiro, Ch. 5-7
Radical Candor by Kim Scott, Ch. 1-2

lecture_notes.introduction: The professional UX environment operates differently from the classroom. Understanding organizational dynamics, cross-functional relationships, and professional norms accelerates your effectiveness.
key_concepts: Adapting academic methods to workplace constraints: faster, lighter, more collaborative
Cross-functional collaboration: working with PMs, engineers, and stakeholders
Design critique culture: giving and receiving feedback professionally
Organizational politics: understanding decision-making structures and influence
Time management: balancing multiple projects and stakeholder expectations
lecture_notes.demonstration: Group discussion: students share their first-week observations and challenges, with faculty and experienced students providing guidance

review.value: Academic: 20 participants, 4-week study, full report. Professional: 5 participants, 3 days, verbal readout. Both are valid — professional practice optimizes for speed and actionability within real constraints.

Professional critiques focus on whether the design meets user needs and business goals, not personal aesthetic preferences. Frame feedback as: 'How does this serve the user's goal of X?' not 'I think the color should be different.'

learning_objectives: Assess progress against learning goals and adjust as needed
Begin documenting internship projects for portfolio inclusion
Address challenges and roadblocks with faculty guidance
readings: Show Your Work by Austin Kleon, Ch. 1-3
How to document your work for a UX portfolio (NNg article)

lecture_notes.introduction: Halfway through the internship, it is time to assess progress, address challenges, and begin the critical task of documenting your work for your portfolio.
key_concepts: Learning goal progress assessment: on track, ahead, behind, and pivot strategies
Real-time documentation: capturing artifacts, screenshots, and process photos throughout
Confidentiality navigation: what can and cannot go in your portfolio from work projects
NDA-compliant case studies: how to show process without revealing proprietary details
Internship supervisor feedback integration: acting on formal and informal feedback
lecture_notes.demonstration: Portfolio documentation workshop: students bring internship artifacts and practice writing case study narratives that respect confidentiality while showing their process

review.value: Many internship projects are confidential. Solutions: (1) Get written permission for specific artifacts. (2) Anonymize the client/product. (3) Show process (research methods, wireframes) without showing final proprietary designs. (4) Focus narrative on your thinking and skills, not the client's product.

Do not wait until the internship ends to document. Capture screenshots, take photos of whiteboards, save early drafts, and write brief notes weekly. Reconstructing process months later is nearly impossible.

learning_objectives: Extract industry insights from the internship experience
Build professional relationships for long-term career benefit
Identify skill gaps revealed by professional practice
readings: Never Eat Alone by Keith Ferrazzi, Ch. 1-3 (on professional networking)
The Making of a Manager by Julie Zhuo, Ch. 7-8

lecture_notes.introduction: The internship provides unique access to industry professionals, processes, and culture. Maximizing this learning opportunity extends far beyond your assigned projects.
key_concepts: Informational interviews with senior designers at your company
Observing design culture: how design decisions are made, who has influence, what is valued
Identifying mentors and building lasting professional relationships
Skill gap analysis: what the internship revealed you need to learn
Industry trends observed firsthand vs. academic theory
lecture_notes.demonstration: Students present their most significant internship learning — something they could not have learned in the classroom — followed by group discussion

review.value: Ask senior designers at your company for 20-minute conversations: How did you get here? What skills matter most? What do you wish you had learned earlier? These conversations provide career guidance no class can match.

Observe how design decisions are really made: Who has the final say? How are trade-offs resolved? What evidence convinces stakeholders? Understanding organizational dynamics is as important as technical skills.

learning_objectives: Complete internship deliverables and knowledge transfer
Present internship learnings and portfolio artifacts to the class
Create a post-internship career action plan
readings: What Color Is Your Parachute? by Richard Bolles, Ch. on Self-Assessment
Range by David Epstein, Ch. 1 (on career breadth)

lecture_notes.introduction: The final phase of the internship focuses on leaving a positive impression, transferring knowledge, and translating the experience into career momentum.
key_concepts: Professional exit: knowledge transfer, documentation, and thank-you notes
Internship presentation: sharing learnings with the class community
Portfolio integration: turning internship projects into polished case studies
Post-internship career action plan: job search strategy, skill development, networking follow-up
Requesting references and maintaining professional relationships
lecture_notes.demonstration: Internship showcase: each student presents their most significant internship contribution, key learnings, and how the experience shaped their career direction
setup_requirements: Portfolio case study draft from internship

review.value: Leave your internship better than you found it: document your work, organize files, write handoff notes for whoever continues your projects. This professionalism leads to return offers and strong references.

The internship is a launchpad, not a destination. Within 2 weeks of completion: update your portfolio, connect with colleagues on LinkedIn, send thank-you notes to mentors, and begin the next step of your career plan.

major_assignments.title: Internship Learning Portfolio
major_assignments.description: Document your internship experience through a structured learning portfolio including weekly reflections, project documentation, supervisor evaluations, and a comprehensive internship case study for your professional portfolio.
objectives: Maintain weekly reflection journals documenting learning and growth
Document internship projects with appropriate confidentiality handling
Obtain and respond to supervisor and peer feedback throughout
Produce a portfolio-ready case study from the internship experience
deliverables: Weekly reflection journals (15 entries) documenting learnings, challenges, and growth
Internship project documentation (NDA-compliant) with process artifacts
Supervisor evaluation form (completed by internship supervisor)
Self-assessment against original SMART learning goals
Portfolio case study from the internship experience (NDA-compliant)
Final internship presentation (15 minutes) to faculty and peers

criteria: Reflections are substantive and demonstrate genuine learning
Challenges are honestly documented with strategies for addressing them
Connections drawn between academic learning and professional practice
Growth is visible across the 15-week arc
Projects documented with appropriate process detail
Confidentiality handled professionally
Your role and contributions clearly articulated
Design decisions documented with rationale
Supervisor provides positive or constructive evaluation
Intern demonstrates professional behavior and growth
Feedback indicates meaningful contribution to the team
Self-assessment aligns with supervisor assessment
Case study follows narrative arc structure
Process is visible while respecting confidentiality
Impact or contribution is clearly stated
Writing is professional and portfolio-ready
Presentation clearly communicates key learnings
Internship experience is contextualized for the audience
Professional growth is demonstrated
Recommendations for future interns are included
Substantive weekly reflections showing genuine learning arc
Well-documented projects respecting confidentiality
Positive supervisor evaluation demonstrating professional growth
Portfolio-ready case study and compelling presentation

weekly_reflections.description: Weekly journal entries documenting learning, challenges, and professional growth

project_documentation.description: Documentation of internship projects with process artifacts and design rationale

supervisor_evaluation.description: Evaluation from internship supervisor assessing professional performance and growth

portfolio_case_study.description: NDA-compliant portfolio case study from the internship experience

final_presentation.description: Presentation of internship learnings and portfolio artifacts to faculty and peers

course_policies.attendance: Attend all scheduled faculty check-ins (5 total across 15 weeks). Report any internship attendance issues to both supervisor and faculty advisor immediately.
course_policies.late_submissions: Weekly reflections are due by Sunday each week. Late reflections lose 15% per day. Final deliverables have a firm deadline.
course_policies.collaboration: Internship work follows employer policies. Academic deliverables (reflections, case study) must be individual work.
course_policies.academic_integrity: Accurately represent your contributions and learning. Do not inflate your role in team projects. Supervisor evaluation provides independent verification of your claims.
course_policies.technology_requirements: Internship journal platform (Notion or Google Docs), portfolio platform for case study, presentation software, and tools as required by the internship employer.
